definition: Deathless language for the consummation side of the path.
notes: The project prefers deathless because it is the most established and least distorting English choice in doctrinal contexts. Death-free remains a controlled alternate when the privative structure needs to stay more explicit, and deathlessness is allowed where the text turns the adjective into a descriptive doctrinal noun. Buddhadasa explicitly uses amata as spiritual deathlessness rather than personal immortality, and Dhp 21 supports the practical amatapada formula that keeps this family live in translator-facing work. In this repository, amata is not the same as nibbāna: nibbāna remains the governing consummation-side headword, while amata highlights one facet of that consummation, namely its deathless character. It also differs from asaṅkhata, which highlights the unconditioned character rather than the deathless one. Translators should therefore resist flattening amata into immortality, eternal life, or a soul-like survival claim.

context_rules.context: In most doctrinal and practical contexts
In explanatory prose clarifying the privative structure
context_rules.notes: Use this as a controlled alternate when the English line benefits from directness.

context_rules.context: When the line turns the descriptor into a doctrinal noun or path-goal expression
context_rules.notes: Use this as a controlled alternate when the sentence needs a noun rather than an adjective.

context_rules.context: In amatapada compounds and related formulas
context_rules.notes: Keep the deathless family stable rather than substituting nibbāna or unconditioned language.

example_phrases.translation: heedfulness is the path to the deathless
example_phrases.notes: Shows the classic practical formula. Keep the deathless logic explicit.

authority_basis.scope: Supports treating amata as spiritual deathlessness rather than personal immortality.
Supports the path-to-the-deathless formula in amatapada contexts.

translation_policy.default_scope: Use 'deathless' as the default rendering in the entry's governed doctrinal and formula contexts unless a recorded context rule overrides it.
translation_policy.when_not_to_apply: Use 'death-free' or 'deathlessness' only when the sentence clearly benefits. Do not flatten amata into immortality, eternal-self language, or generic nibbāna-language.
translation_policy.drift_risk: Prevents the deathless family from collapsing into eternalist, soul-like, or generic consummation language.
